name: Root-to-Node Path Comparison
summary: Find the full root-to-p path and root-to-q path by DFS, then walk both from the root; the last node they share is the lowest common ancestor. Works on any binary tree — it never uses the BST ordering.

lineExplanations.3: Read how many slots the level-order array has.
lineExplanations.4: nodes will hold one dict per real node — this is what the stage draws.
lineExplanations.5: by_id maps a node id to its dict for O(1) lookups.
lineExplanations.6: state_by_id[i] tracks how the stage should colour node i.
lineExplanations.7: Give every slot a starting colour state of 0 (idle).
lineExplanations.8: Append one idle-state entry per slot.
lineExplanations.9: child walks the array to hand out left/right children in level order.
lineExplanations.10: Rebuild the tree: visit each array slot in order.
lineExplanations.11: Only real values become nodes; null slots are skipped.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node's dict with its id, value, and empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: If a slot remains, it is this node's left child.
lineExplanations.14: A non-null slot means a real left child exists.
lineExplanations.15: Link the left child by its array index.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the child walker past the left slot.
lineExplanations.17: If another slot remains, it is this node's right child.
lineExplanations.18: A non-null slot means a real right child exists.
lineExplanations.19: Link the right child by its array index.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the child walker past the right slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the drawn node list.
lineExplanations.22: Register the node in by_id for O(1) lookups.
lineExplanations.24: path_p will collect the root-to-p node ids.
lineExplanations.25: Run a DFS from the root to fill path_p with the path to value p.
lineExplanations.26: Clear every colour state before the second search.
lineExplanations.27: Reset slot i back to idle.
lineExplanations.28: path_q will collect the root-to-q node ids.
lineExplanations.29: Run a DFS from the root to fill path_q with the path to value q.
lineExplanations.30: Clear every colour state again before drawing the two paths.
lineExplanations.31: Reset slot i back to idle.
lineExplanations.32: Paint every node on the path to p.
lineExplanations.33: Mark node path_p[i] as being on p's path (state 5).
lineExplanations.34: Paint every node on the path to q.
lineExplanations.35: Mark node path_q[i] as being on q's path (state 6).
lineExplanations.36: lca will hold the answer value; -1 is a placeholder.
lineExplanations.37: lca_id remembers which node is the current lowest shared ancestor.
lineExplanations.38: depth walks both paths together from the root.
lineExplanations.39: Compare the two paths position by position while both still have nodes.
lineExplanations.40: Read the node on p's path at this depth.
lineExplanations.41: Read the node on q's path at this depth.
lineExplanations.42: Do both paths pass through the SAME node at this depth?
lineExplanations.43: Mark this shared node as part of the common trunk (state 4).
lineExplanations.44: Remember it as the lowest shared ancestor so far.
lineExplanations.45: Record its value as the current LCA candidate.
lineExplanations.46: Move one step deeper along both paths.
lineExplanations.48: The paths diverge here — the previous shared node was the LCA.
lineExplanations.52: A None id means we walked off the tree — dead end.
lineExplanations.53: Report failure back up the recursion.
lineExplanations.54: Add this node to the current DFS path.
lineExplanations.55: Highlight this node as the one we are visiting.
lineExplanations.56: Is this node the target we are searching for?
lineExplanations.57: Found it — report success; the path now ends at the target.
lineExplanations.58: Look up this node's left child id.
lineExplanations.59: Recurse into the left subtree to search for the target.
lineExplanations.60: The target was found on the left — report success.
lineExplanations.61: Look up this node's right child id.
lineExplanations.62: Recurse into the right subtree to search for the target.
lineExplanations.63: The target was found on the right — report success.
lineExplanations.64: Dead end on both sides — remove this node from the path (backtrack).
lineExplanations.66: Report failure so the caller keeps searching.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — LeetCode's standard wrapper class.
syntaxExplanations.2: def lowestCommonAncestor(self, values, p, q): — values is the tree; p and q are the two target node values.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the array slots (including nulls).
syntaxExplanations.5: {} creates an empty dictionary (a hash map).
syntaxExplanations.6: Another empty list, for per-node colour states.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: .append(0) adds a 0 to the end of the list.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 — the root is slot 0, so children start at slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass to build the nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks the slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal stores this node's fields; None means 'no child yet'.
syntaxExplanations.13: child < n guards against reading past the array end.
syntaxExplanations.14: Check whether the left-child slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's index.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 moves the walker forward.
syntaxExplanations.17: Again check there is a slot for the right child.
syntaxExplanations.18: Check whether the right-child slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's index.
syntaxExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right slot.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds this node to the drawn list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node keys the node by its id.
syntaxExplanations.23: 0 if n > 0 else None — a ternary picking the root id.
syntaxExplanations.24: [] — an empty list to collect the path to p.
syntaxExplanations.25: self.findPath(...) calls the recursive DFS helper.
syntaxExplanations.26: for i in range(n): loop over every slot.
syntaxExplanations.27: state_by_id[i] = 0 resets slot i to idle.
syntaxExplanations.28: [] — an empty list to collect the path to q.
syntaxExplanations.29: self.findPath(...) runs the DFS again, this time for q.
syntaxExplanations.30: for i in range(n): loop over every slot.
syntaxExplanations.31: state_by_id[i] = 0 resets slot i to idle.
syntaxExplanations.32: range(len(path_p)) iterates over p's path positions.
syntaxExplanations.34: range(len(path_q)) iterates over q's path positions.
syntaxExplanations.37: lca_id = -1 — no shared node identified yet.
syntaxExplanations.38: depth = 0 — start comparing from the root.
syntaxExplanations.39: and requires BOTH paths to still have a node at this depth.
syntaxExplanations.42: == tests whether the two path nodes are identical.
syntaxExplanations.44: lca_id = a stores the current lowest shared node id.
syntaxExplanations.45: by_id[a]["value"] reads the shared node's value.
syntaxExplanations.46: depth = depth + 1 advances both paths one level.
syntaxExplanations.47: else: the paths differ at this depth.
syntaxExplanations.49: return lca hands back the answer.
syntaxExplanations.51: def findPath(self, node_id, target, path, ...): the recursive DFS; nodes/by_id/state_by_id are threaded in so the stage can draw during recursion.
syntaxExplanations.52: is None checks whether we walked off the tree.
syntaxExplanations.53: return False signals 'target not in this branch'.
syntaxExplanations.54: path.append(node_id) records this node on the current path.
syntaxExplanations.56: == compares this node's stored value with the target.
syntaxExplanations.57: return True signals 'target found — path is complete'.
syntaxExplanations.58: by_id[node_id]["left"] is the left child id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.59: A recursive call that searches the left subtree.
syntaxExplanations.60: return True bubbles success up the call stack.
syntaxExplanations.61: by_id[node_id]["right"] is the right child id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.62: A recursive call that searches the right subtree.
syntaxExplanations.63: return True bubbles success up the call stack.
syntaxExplanations.64: path.pop() removes this node — it led nowhere (backtrack).
syntaxExplanations.66: return False reports the target was not under this node.

visualizationIntent: init: rebuild and show the whole BST. path-p: DFS from the root, lighting the live path and dimming dead-ends, until p is reached. path-q: reset, then DFS again to reach q. compare: paint p's path and q's path in two colours, then walk both from the root; each shared node joins the trunk and becomes the LCA candidate. return: the last shared node glows gold as the LCA.
